scope: Current Vibelytics public PNG brand, social, product, campaign, favicon, and screenshot assets in the static landing repo.
policy: Public, external, generated, third-party, or licensed brand assets must have source, usage rights, allowed uses, attribution, replacement decision, and open rights questions recorded before the brand system can be called production-ready.
overallDecision: The three P0 route-used campaign/product PNGs are now first-party deterministic assets generated from repo source. No current PNG is approved as canonical logo, favicon, social, or production brand source.

requiredBeforeProductionBrandSignoff: Export favicon, app icon, avatar, social, and brand PNG derivatives only from approved source assets.
Refresh screenshots after route-used image replacements are accepted.
canonicalSourceGate.doNotCreateYet: Do not create brand/vibelytics-mark.svg until replacement direction is accepted or provenance is resolved enough to proceed.

assets.source: Repo-local PNG added in commit b7c4853. Exact generation/source file unknown.
assets.allowedUses: Temporary repo asset and internal brand evidence only. Do not expand to new public, deck, social, or merchandise usage.
assets.decision: Preserve the rounded-square V mark concept as evidence, but replace this raster with approved SVG-derived exports.
assets.replacementRecommendation: Create deterministic brand/vibelytics-mark.svg after the asset-source gate is accepted; export future mark PNGs from that SVG.
openQuestions: Was this generated, hand-designed, or exported from a source file?
Is there an editable SVG/vector source outside the repo?
Can the current raster be used publicly across website, social, deck, and merchandise?

assets.source: Repo-local PNG added in commit b7c4853 and modified in commit 27fe263. Appears composed from brand mark, text, and event imagery. Exact source and rights unknown.
assets.allowedUses: Internal evidence only until source and embedded image rights are documented.
assets.decision: Replace before future social or brand implementation.
assets.replacementRecommendation: Rebuild from the approved SVG mark plus first-party or documented campaign imagery; keep public copy pure Vibelytics and avoid SR007/Speedrun language on public social previews.
openQuestions: What is the provenance of the event image embedded in the composition?
What tool or source file produced this asset?
Is public social distribution approved?

assets.source: Repo-local PNG added in commit b7c4853. Exact generation/source file unknown.
assets.allowedUses: Internal evidence only until source and social usage rights are documented.
assets.decision: Replace with SVG-derived avatar export before future social implementation.
assets.replacementRecommendation: Export from approved mark source with documented background/grid treatment.
openQuestions: Was this exported from the same source as the mark?
Is X/social profile usage approved?

assets.source: Repo-local PNG added in commit b7c4853 and modified in commit 27fe263. Exact generation/source file unknown.
assets.allowedUses: Internal campaign evidence only until embedded image and composition rights are documented.
assets.decision: Replace before future social header use.
assets.replacementRecommendation: Rebuild as a Signal Desk social header from approved mark source and documented imagery or first-party product graphics.
openQuestions: What imagery or generated outputs are embedded?
What source file produced the composition?
Is X/social header usage approved?

assets.source: Repo-local PNG added in commit b7c4853. Likely derivative of brand/vibelytics-mark.png; exact source unknown.
assets.allowedUses: Current deployed temporary favicon only. Do not treat as approved production export.
assets.decision: Replace after canonical SVG mark is approved.
assets.replacementRecommendation: Export from approved brand/vibelytics-mark.svg with small-size simplification and document export settings.
openQuestions: Was this generated directly from the current mark PNG?
Are current mark rights approved for deployed favicon use?

assets.source: First-party deterministic replacement generated from scripts/generate-route-assets.py on 2026-07-03. The composition is an abstract stage and launch-signal network; it uses no photos, external assets, people, venue likeness, or third-party sources.
assets.usageRights: Repo-owned deterministic output. Approved for current Vibelytics route use and internal docs.
assets.allowedUses: Current `/` hero implementation, internal docs, and future Vibelytics campaign drafts. Re-export from the generator before resizing or adapting.
assets.decision: Replacement implemented. Preserve as the current route-used hero image source until a broader visual system pass replaces or extends it.
assets.replacementRecommendation: No source-rights replacement needed. Future visual refinements should update scripts/generate-route-assets.py and regenerate this PNG.
openQuestions: Visual direction can still be refined in a later design pass.
Do not use this as a substitute for canonical logo, favicon, or social preview source assets.

assets.source: First-party deterministic replacement generated from scripts/generate-route-assets.py on 2026-07-03. The composition is an abstract city-fit/product signal diagram; it uses no maps, photos, external assets, people, venue likeness, or third-party sources.
assets.usageRights: Repo-owned deterministic output. Approved for current Vibelytics route use and internal docs.
assets.allowedUses: Current `/` product section, current `/pilot` workspace image, internal docs, and future Vibelytics product/campaign drafts. Re-export from the generator before resizing or adapting.
assets.decision: Replacement implemented. Preserve as the current route-used product/city-fit image source until a broader visual system pass replaces or extends it.
assets.replacementRecommendation: No source-rights replacement needed. Future visual refinements should update scripts/generate-route-assets.py and regenerate this PNG.
openQuestions: Visual direction can still be refined in a later design pass.
Continue to avoid live audience tracking, heatmap, or surveillance metaphors in surrounding copy and future variants.

assets.source: First-party deterministic replacement generated from scripts/generate-route-assets.py on 2026-07-03. The composition is an abstract backstage/pre-show decision surface; it uses no photos, external assets, people, venue likeness, or third-party sources.
assets.usageRights: Repo-owned deterministic output. Approved for current Vibelytics route use and internal docs.
assets.allowedUses: Current `/` narrative section, internal docs, and future Vibelytics campaign drafts. Re-export from the generator before resizing or adapting.
assets.decision: Replacement implemented. Preserve as the current route-used backstage/pre-show image source until a broader visual system pass replaces or extends it.
assets.replacementRecommendation: No source-rights replacement needed. Future visual refinements should update scripts/generate-route-assets.py and regenerate this PNG.

assets.source: Repo-local PNG added in commit 6ef78b5. Exact source unknown.
assets.allowedUses: Internal evidence only. It is not referenced by current routes.
assets.decision: Remove from future brand implementation unless provenance is resolved; it can remain in the repo as historical evidence for now.
assets.replacementRecommendation: If a control-room visual is needed, create or license a fresh Signal Desk image with documented rights.
openQuestions: Was this image generated, licensed, or photographed?
Does this asset fit the approved Signal Desk territory better than current route imagery?
Can it be publicly reused without attribution?

assets.source: Repo-local PNG added in commit 6ef78b5. Exact source unknown.
assets.allowedUses: Internal evidence only. It is not referenced by current routes.
assets.decision: Remove from future brand implementation and replace with current route screenshots or source-controlled first-party product mockups.

Does it still represent the current pilot accurately?
Can it be publicly reused without attribution?

assets.source: Captured from repo implementation; originally added in commit 6ef78b5 and modified in commit 4c7b9b9.
assets.usageRights: Derivative of repo-local implementation and current assets; inherits open asset provenance questions.

assets.decision: Safe only as internal evidence. Regenerate after public assets are replaced.
assets.replacementRecommendation: Capture fresh screenshots after the landing page is updated with documented assets.
openQuestions: Screenshot contains current route assets whose provenance is still unknown.

nextAction: A future Codex thread should create the canonical SVG mark and export derivative favicon, app icon, brand, social, and screenshot assets from approved sources. Do not claim production-ready brand signoff until the P1 identity/export gates are closed.
